module_mood_contract: Awe and curiosity — the 'oh shit, where is this all going?' moment. Agents building agents, capability compounding, no visible ceiling. The student leaves wanting the next move, not feeling done. Any edit that makes M8 feel like a tidy graduation ceremony steals this.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module's single lecture is a 5-min demo ('Agent generates agent') placed at the opener to show a ceiling — not a meta-frame naming a pattern. The identity-naming close is the meta-frame moment, placed last. Big Idea and Key Concepts arrive after exercises. Lecture placement is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises
rules_evaluated.evidence: The opener demo (~5 min) arms the student to do (shows the ceiling), then both exercises follow. No framework mapping, no named practitioner terms, no why-X-beats-Y explanation precedes the exercises. The three thinking-disciplines (crux, assumption-test, pre-mortem) are named in Key Concepts AFTER exercises — correctly placed. Dosage test: the opener teaches nothing, it shows. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+): student leaves chair while agent works
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Joint Double Diamond by design has agents running the heavy lifting (stance → cross-check → proposal → synthesizer → midway-injection → critique → pushback) while the student sits in the room and steers. The Debrief prompt is a long-running rewrite. Both are walk-away agentic beats. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Exercise quality ladder — M8 awe exercises should target 9-design
rules_evaluated.evidence: M8 is one of the four identified 9-design exercises (M1 joy, M5 rescue, M6 leverage, M8 awe). The Joint Double Diamond has re-runnable artifact (strategy kernel), traveling vocabulary (flywheel, crux), awe moment (twenty agents ran an argument). The Extend exercise is an 8-supporting beat by design. Ladder honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide, no forced phases or scripted reactions
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module body does not prescribe the student's emotional reactions. Push-back in the Debrief is student-initiated ('Push back where it's wrong'). The Next section describes moves without commanding them. The identity-naming close is the sponsor's statement, not a command to feel a certain way. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: Re-fire check: the prior REVISE was that the callout 'Vague summaries are how next round inherits this round's blur' read as a hygiene admonition in body prose (a forcing-function concern outside the fence, unreachable by speed-runners). The replacement callout — 'The flywheel amplifies specificity the same way it amplifies everything else. Precision compounds. So does blur.' — is NOT a forcing function at all. It is an awe-register explanatory observation about WHY the prompt insists on specificity. The actual forcing function (before-and-after quote requirement) lives inside the fenced prompt block and remains intact. The body callout now serves its legitimate purpose (mood reinforcement / explanatory prose), not a forcing-function purpose it was wrongly placed for. The Debrief prompt's 'for each claimed change, quote the before-and-after line or section header so I can confirm it landed' is the load-bearing forcing function, inside the fence. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
rules_evaluated.evidence: No host-skill install path anywhere in the student-facing body. The Debrief prompt is addressed to 'Builder Claude' — a runtime label, not a skill name. Push-back moves are natural body prose and trainer-narrated. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: The single fenced prompt block (Debrief) contains no [BRACKET] placeholders. All references are to session artifacts that exist at this point (shared deliberation folder, challenge.md, context manifests, stances, etc.). Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt is preceded by 'Five minutes. Claude reviews the session and sharpens whichever file carried the load: the central synthesizer's rules, the strategy kernel, the agent set, the plan, or the rules that governed how the agents argued.' This is a semantic summary with a command verb ('reviews... sharpens'). Acceptable lead-in.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block
rules_evaluated.evidence: The prompt block is labeled '**Prompt** *(Builder Claude)*' with a blank line before the fence. No 'copy → ' prefix. No '*(end of prompt)*' closer. The destination chip names Builder Claude. Canonical shape honored.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No markdown bold/italic inside fenced prompt blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt uses plain text inside the fence. No asterisk-wrapped bold or italic. Bullets and plain emphasis through word choice only. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Overwrite anxiety — name the overwrite default on regeneration
rules_evaluated.evidence: The Debrief prompt explicitly says: 'Identify the file path and confirm in one line before rewriting; overwrite in place, do not create a parallel version.' Overwrite behavior named. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — every module has a deliberate mood and hand-off
rules_evaluated.evidence: The M8 mood contract (awe and curiosity, forward hunger, not closure) is honored across the module arc. After re-fire fixes, all five phases score 8 or above. The opener lands at 9, the Diamond exercise at 9, Phase 4 (Key Concepts + Debrief) now at 9, the identity-naming close at 9. The hand-off goes to debrief and training close, not to a next module — correct for the training closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module's mood is generative, not corrective. The student does not leave feeling they built wrong things. The walls bullet now reads as capability confirmation, not failure-naming. The callout is awe-register (flywheel law applied to precision), not admonitory. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea is 'The tool that builds tools compounds.' Positive discipline. Key Concepts lead with self-improvement, org capability, portable thinking disciplines. The grounding bullet names a discipline ('publish what it read, what it couldn't find, and what it inferred without a source'), not a failure class. The callout reinforces the flywheel law, not a failure mode. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream teaching moments
rules_evaluated.evidence: M8 is the training closer. No downstream module to front-run. The three thinking-disciplines (crux, assumption-test, pre-mortem) are named in Key Concepts here — they were introduced in M7 and re-applied at room scale in M8, consistent with the strategy-doc sequencing. No sequencing violations detected.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.evidence: Agents 101 recurring themes: compounding, judgment, trust-distance, system-not-demo, org-capability. Key Concepts bullets instance: self-improvement (compounding), org capability (org-capability theme), domain prediction framework (judgment theme applied to the 200-process landscape), thinking disciplines as portable named moves (judgment theme), grounding-when-agents-read-agents (trust-distance theme — explicit citation/sourcing requirement). At least three theme instances. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone themes section
rules_evaluated.evidence: No standalone 'Themes this module instances:' section. Key Concepts are tactical bullets, module-voiced, no (theme N) notation. The walls and domain-prediction bullets are pure tactical. The org-capability and self-improvement bullets end with theme-resonant phrasing but don't use strategy-doc vocabulary. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Callout register — awe vs. hygiene-discipline at the close (re-fire)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prior REVISE: 'Vague summaries are how next round inherits this round's blur' read in hygiene-discipline register — a cautionary admonition in the awe close. Fix applied: callout rewritten to 'The flywheel amplifies specificity the same way it amplifies everything else. Precision compounds. So does blur.' This is awe register: the flywheel law applied to what gets written into it — imprecision compounds the same way capability does. Not a hygiene checklist. Not an admonition. The same compounding law that explains the wins also explains why blur is dangerous. SVP persona reads this as awe-amplifying, not cautionary. The wrap sentence ('The flywheel that sharpened the file just now is the one that will sharpen it again after Agents 101...') layers on top of a callout already in awe register. Phase 4 mood no longer interrupted at this beat. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walls bullet register — capability-emphasis vs. pragmatic-planning interrupt (re-fire)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prior REVISE: 'Three predictable walls after Agents 101: data access, runtime platform, discoverability. The plan you just wrote names which wall bites first.' — pragmatic-planning register, mood interrupt after the Diamond 9-peak. Fix applied: bullet now reads 'The plan you produced already names which wall bites first for your company. That you can see them is the sign the kernel works.' The sentence 'That you can see them is the sign the kernel works' reframes visibility of the walls as a capability signal — the plan is good enough to know where friction lands first, rather than here is a list of things to worry about. SVP persona reads this twice; it lands as confirmation ('what we built is real enough to name this') rather than as a warning list. Mood register preserved. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context manifest jargon-before-earned — behavioral description replaces unearned term (re-fire)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prior REVISE: 'context manifest' appeared in Key Concepts but nowhere in the exercise prompts the student ran — jargon planted before it was earned, causing the SVP persona to slow. Fix applied: Key Concepts bullet now reads 'Every agent publishes what it read, what it couldn't find, and what it inferred without a source. Every claim cites the file it came from.' No new term introduced. The discipline is conveyed through behavioral description — three categories (read / couldn't find / inferred without source) — not via a labeled concept. The SVP persona recognizes this immediately because she just watched the cross-agent hallucination risk materialize in the Diamond exercise. The description maps directly to what she observed. No jargon to decode. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: M8 is the closer — hand-off goes to debrief / training close, not next module
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module correctly closes the training arc. The Next section bridges to life after Agents 101 ('The plan you just produced is a set of assumptions dressed as work. Label them. Start the concurrent pieces...') not to a Module 9. 'Agents 101 is Step 1 of 5 (Make Your Own is next)' names the forward path without over-promising. Hand-off is to the student's own work, not the curriculum. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M8 awe is the meta-platform reveal, not a closure
rules_evaluated.evidence: The identity-naming close ('You don't graduate. You have a flywheel.') is explicitly non-ceremonial. The module body says 'No certificate. No graduation. A name the graduate can say, back at work, to colleagues who weren't there.' The Next section adds 'You don't graduate. You have a flywheel.' The mood is forward hunger, not closure. The awe is not resolved — it's generative. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Three Predictable Walls bullet — register interrupt risk at the awe close (re-fire)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prior REVISE: walls bullet named problems coming, interrupting awe close. Fix applied: 'That you can see them is the sign the kernel works.' The register shift is confirmed: the walls are now a capability-verification statement, not a planning list. The student reads this as evidence that the strategy kernel is real enough to name specific friction points — not as a warning about what's ahead. No mood interrupt detected in re-fired persona simulation. PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — self-improvement and org-capability
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two teaching moments are named in the strategy doc: (1) self-improvement (each cycle sharpens the next), (2) org capability (20 people produced a strategy kernel no consultant could deliver). Both appear in Key Concepts. The Debrief prompt surfaces the self-improvement mechanism literally. The persona sim confirms both land — the 'twenty agents ran an argument' awe moment is exactly the org-capability teaching moment. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — The tool that builds tools compounds
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'The tool that builds tools compounds.' The module body delivers this literally (agent generates agent in the demo, Extend exercise has the student's coding agent build a new retriever, Diamond has agents cross-checking and building on each other's proposals). The Key Concepts self-improvement bullet restates it through the session's own evidence. The identity-naming close grounds it: 'You can do it again on the next problem.' Big Idea is earned through doing, not asserted through prose. Pass.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Phase boundaries at pause beats — designed conversation pauses
rules_evaluated.evidence: The module has natural pause beats: after the demo (trainer narrates, room processes), between Extend and Diamond (natural transition), during Diamond at midway-instructions injection (explicit human decision layer), after Debrief prompt run (reading Claude's summary is a designed pause), at identity-naming close. For an M8 capstone, the pause structure is appropriate. Pass.

phase_scores.note: Opener prose achieves forward-leaning, not graduation. Demo dosage is correct.
Multiplication felt, quiet awe. Setup beat for Diamond.
Awe peak. Twenty agents ran an argument. Org-capability teaching moment lands.

All three prior mood interrupters resolved. Walls bullet: capability-emphasis ('that you can see them is the sign the kernel works'). Grounding bullet: behavioral description without unearned jargon. Callout: awe register (flywheel amplifies specificity — the law applies to itself). No hygiene-discipline register. No pragmatic-planning interrupt. Phase 4 now sustains awe from the Diamond peak.
Forward hunger complete. 'You don't graduate. You have a flywheel.' is the correct close.

todos.note: First learning goal says 'Use Claude Code to generate a new agent.' Runtime-fork or neutralize for Cowork so the goal remains 'use the runtime to generate a new agent' without making Cowork feel like a secondary path. Tracked in maintainer block — not a storytelling-class blocker.
